requirements.acceptance: A canonical trust-anchored snapshot rejects mutable worktree authority, embedded/self-signed key substitution, missing hashes, unmanifested artifacts, archive/path escape, and ambiguous contract identity.
requirements.implementation_action: Define the v2 producer snapshot binding clean source, exact wire contracts, canonical encoding/domain separation, API, package, ontology, node pack, wheel, capabilities, certificates, revocation index, signed journal bootstrap/checkpoint, and artifacts by hash.
requirements.title: MaskFactory Immutable Integration Release Snapshot

requirements.acceptance: Only a fully verified named release signed by an allowed active key and bound to the current revocation index/journal head can become the active Main producer pin; prior non-revoked pins remain recoverable.
requirements.implementation_action: Implement isolated import, safe archive extraction, content hashing, out-of-band trusted-key verification, canonical payload/signature verification, clean-source verification, atomic pinning, checkpoint validation, and rollback metadata.
requirements.title: Main Exact Release Pin and Integrity Verifier

requirements.acceptance: Same-name, semver-compatible-but-hash-different, required-field, enum, or invariant drift fails closed with a typed blocker before normalization or execution.
requirements.implementation_action: Compare schema source, schema ID, semantic version, content hash, required fields, semantic invariants, executable field mapping, API/OpenAPI, package, ontology, node-pack, wheel, certificate format, canonicalization profile, and journal format.
requirements.title: Schema Source Version Hash and Semantic Drift Gate

requirements.acceptance: A revoked, expired, superseded, forked, or invalidated scope cannot be resurrected by cache, rollback, restart, timestamp manipulation, embedded keys, or journal resealing.
requirements.implementation_action: Process supersession, signing-key state, certificate validity, revocation-index freshness, and per-scope invalidation before authorizing a new pin; retain immutable signed adoption and checkpoint evidence.
requirements.title: Release Supersession Revocation and Adoption Boundary

requirements.acceptance: Unknown fields, replayed authorization, target/protected ambiguity, output-as-input collisions, and invented references fail; access mode remains independent from minimum authority and accepted issuers.
requirements.implementation_action: Compile project/run/job/pass/attempt/hypothesis, idempotency/nonce, exact still-frame-or-span media scope, source, declared scene roster, one target, protected character/prop/environment regions, separate input ROI hashes, intent, executable transform, compatibility, accepted claim class, resource/deadline, authority, and policy fields from immutable pass records.
requirements.title: Strict v2 Bridge Request Compiler

requirements.acceptance: Mode A never implies certified and Main performs no producer mutation.
requirements.implementation_action: Resolve packages only through the active pin and verify artifact, source, owner, ontology, transform, authority, and certificate bindings.
requirements.title: Mode A Read-Only Package Adapter

requirements.acceptance: Mode B defaults to draft; a network ambiguity becomes outcome_unknown, never an assumed failure or success, and one result may be stronger only through an active exact serving-route/output operational certificate.
requirements.implementation_action: Implement authenticated health, bounded nonce/deadline/idempotency admission, explicit admitted/submitted/accepted/running/succeeded/failed/cancelled/outcome_unknown lifecycle, predict/refine, reconciliation before resubmission, response validation, route/runtime lineage, and typed failure behavior.
requirements.title: Mode B Live Predict and Refine Adapter

requirements.acceptance: Normalization records factual execution without granting authority; a newer draft cannot overwrite stronger valid authority, close candidates branch only within budget, and ambiguity abstains.
requirements.implementation_action: Normalize both modes while retaining exact input ROI versus generated-output identity, source/media scope, package/service, route and eligible alternatives, runtime kind/digest, timing/resources, hypothesis, owner, transform, authority/claim class, certificate, QA, cache, and blocker lineage.
requirements.title: Normalized Result and Deterministic Arbitration

requirements.acceptance: Unknown values fail; operationally_certified_artifact can support only its exact permitted use and never becomes independent accuracy or training gold; legacy/UI/LLM summaries are non-authoritative.
requirements.implementation_action: Implement exact authority_state, issuer_kind, and claim_class vocabulary with access-mode-independent intended-use policy; migrate the legacy live QA strictness dial/string promotion gates to pinned structured v2 policy.
requirements.title: Frozen Authority Issuer Claim-Class and Legacy Migration Crosswalk

requirements.acceptance: Neither Mode A nor Mode B receives authority outside the exact active certificate scope, and embedded/self-signed keys or fixture evidence cannot establish production trust.
requirements.implementation_action: Verify out-of-band trusted signer, signature algorithm, release, capability, access mode, exact execution stack/runtime provenance, source media/frame/output, owner, transform, QA, policy, scope, issuer, issuance/expiry at decision time, and current revocation index.
requirements.title: Exact Trust-Anchored Operational Certificate Verifier

requirements.acceptance: Producer use_eligibility, QA success, certificate presence, or LLM/VLM opinion is never sufficient by itself; every exact policy criterion and Main QA must pass without blockers.
requirements.implementation_action: Recompute use eligibility from the exact signed pinned Main policy using a complete unique criterion vector, declared comparator/threshold, analyzer and evidence hashes, claim class, signer trust, decision timestamp, certificate temporal evaluation, current revocation index, and genuine runtime evidence.
requirements.title: Structured Mask Authority and Downstream Promotion Gate

requirements.acceptance: Only affected target masks/caches/dependents fail closed while unrelated targets and DAG branches continue; tombstoned authority cannot resurrect.
requirements.implementation_action: Apply signer revocation, certificate expiry/revocation, regression, per-target scope/hash drift, media-frame drift, and policy/revocation-index change as durable signed invalidations and re-evaluate dependents.
requirements.title: Certificate Revocation Demotion and Per-Target Invalidation

requirements.acceptance: Duplicate, swapped, absent, cross-character, prop, environment, or ambiguous assignments block the affected pass and cannot be promotion-authoritative.
requirements.implementation_action: Bind the exact target character plus every visible/protected character, prop, and environment instance to the declared scene roster, provider indices where applicable, references, visibility, occlusion, target masks, and protected input regions.
requirements.title: Multi-Entity Scene Roster Owner and Provider Index Binding

requirements.acceptance: Step continuity, sentinel points, bounds, and masks roundtrip within policy; non-invertible operations, wrong side labels, or image/mask transform divergence fail.
requirements.implementation_action: Record and execute typed ordered crop, resize, pad, flip, projection, inverse, interpolation, rounding, source/output dimensions, character-perspective side swaps, per-step hashes, canonical chain hash, and tolerance.
requirements.title: Executable Coordinate Transform and Roundtrip Validator

requirements.acceptance: Only an exact Mode A package selector may intentionally reference the same immutable package mask; protected-region policy is explicit and each derived output is capped at the weakest parent authority/claim scope.
requirements.implementation_action: Keep input ROI/protected constraint artifacts distinct from generated masks, validate ownership/overlap, and record boolean, morphological, feather, crop, and projection derivations with exact parent hashes and parent authority records.
requirements.title: Target Protected Input and Derived Output Lineage Gate

requirements.acceptance: Repair changes only the failed scope and passes regional plus whole-artifact regression or abstains.
requirements.implementation_action: Localize provider disagreement, preserve accepted parents, require a new hypothesis, and bound candidate, attempt, time, and compute budgets.
requirements.title: Disagreement Arbitration and Localized Mask Repair

requirements.acceptance: Stale health never authorizes execution, container routes require image digests, native/venv routes require environment/lock hashes, and current Mode B default authority is draft.
requirements.implementation_action: Publish freshness-bound service, authenticated endpoint, model-route, authority ceiling, native/venv or container runtime provenance, resource envelope, and compatibility observations.
requirements.title: Health Capability Route and Runtime Snapshot

requirements.acceptance: Confirmed effects are not duplicated, impossible/backward transitions fail, quality retries require a new hypothesis, and breakers are exact-route scoped and deadline bounded.
requirements.implementation_action: Separate transport retry from quality repair, bind retries to request payload/idempotency/nonce/deadline, persist explicit execution state transitions, and reconcile outcome_unknown before resubmission.
requirements.title: Lifecycle Idempotency Retry Deadline and Circuit Breaker

requirements.acceptance: Stale, revoked, wrong-frame, or wrong-owner entries cannot resurrect after restart; forensic retention and fixture entries cannot satisfy active production routes.
requirements.implementation_action: Key cache by release/contracts/source/media/owner/input regions/intent/transform/route/runtime/model/authority/claim/policy and persist signed per-target invalidation tombstones.
requirements.title: Content-Addressed Cache and Per-Scope Invalidation Tombstones

requirements.acceptance: Replay verifies sequence/hash/signature/checkpoint continuity, rejects fork/deletion/reorder/reseal/substituted-key history, reconstructs projections, and resumes only safe work without repeating confirmed external effects.
requirements.implementation_action: Persist canonical domain-separated admitted, submitted, accepted, running, outcome_unknown, received, validated, authority, cache, invalidation, repair, incident, and promotion-decision events with causation, previous hashes, signed bootstrap/checkpoints, and trusted head pins.
requirements.title: Signed Append-Only Bridge Event Journal Checkpoints and Recovery

requirements.acceptance: Core requirements explicitly set human anchors and scale/DAZ maturity as optional; embedded keys, conversational memory, and producer use_eligibility have no Main authority.
requirements.implementation_action: Declare exact wire schema name/version/hash, canonicalization/domain profile, trusted-key registry and allowed key IDs/algorithms, auth/nonce/replay policy, API/package/ontology compatibility, labels, person counts, transforms, media scope, access modes, issuer/certificate/claim scope, runtime provenance, latency/resources, and completion profile.
requirements.title: Main Consumer Requirements and Trust Manifest

requirements.acceptance: Partial adoption authorizes only enumerated diagnostic/shadow capabilities and never broad production use; every required signature must resolve to an active out-of-band trusted key.
requirements.implementation_action: Record adopted, partially adopted, or rejected status with exact pin, signed trust result, current revocation index, journal checkpoint/head pin, checks, mismatches, capability scope, and revalidation triggers.
requirements.title: Producer Consumer Adoption Receipt

requirements.acceptance: Any schema, semantic, trust, ontology, route, authority, lifecycle, or certificate drift fails before runtime activation.
requirements.implementation_action: Run producer fixtures and Main positive/negative/adversarial fixtures against the same immutable release and consumer manifest, including canonicalization, signer substitution, nonce replay, path/archive safety, media/ownership/transform drift, certificate time/revocation, journal forks, and mapping completeness.
requirements.title: Bidirectional Compatibility Contract and Adversarial CI

requirements.acceptance: Main requests no gold mutation or authority change; producer response appears only through its own later event/release.
requirements.implementation_action: Return source/result hashes, localized defect, QA evidence, and requested producer action through an append-only request.
requirements.title: Typed Feedback Repair Request Without Truth Mutation

requirements.acceptance: Positive and adversarial fixtures prove Mode A/Mode B authority independence, exact-certificate live eligibility, fixture/runtime separation, and rejection of embedded-key, producer eligibility, conversational summary, live QA-dial, or string-only promotion authority.
requirements.implementation_action: Validate every schema/example, canonical/security invariant, executable producer mapping, trust anchor, signature/time/revocation rule, journal/state-machine rule, dependency, ownership/media/transform/lineage rule, authority/claim rule, structured promotion criterion, legacy migration, completion profile, mirror, and preservation hash.
requirements.title: Strict v2 Fixture Semantic Security and Legacy Migration Validator Suite

requirements.acceptance: Every named page consumes only its registered read models and evidence lineage; Browser, App Mode, LLM, and VLM cannot bypass the adapter, mutate producer truth, commit promotion, or infer runtime readiness from fixtures.
requirements.implementation_action: Implement the explicit read-model map for Home/readiness, Projects/revisions, Scene Builder Pose & Masks, Runs/DAG, Queue/Workers, Recovery, and QA, including the strict readiness-projection v2 contract.
requirements.title: Bridge Observability Incident and App Projection

requirements.acceptance: Every core gate produces trust- and hash-bound genuine runtime evidence; wrong-owner/frame/key/revocation/transform injections fail closed, unrelated branches continue, and an injected child failure preserves its accepted parent.
requirements.implementation_action: Run single-person Mode A, two-person plus prop/environment ownership/protection, still/frame/span scope, Mode B draft and exact certified output, outage/outcome_unknown reconciliation, per-target invalidation, signed-journal restart, and downstream edit proofs.
requirements.title: Single and Multi-Character Integrated Bridge Proof

requirements.acceptance: Row218 and every core bridge gate pass; optional independent accuracy and scale/DAZ maturity are reported separately and do not block core.
requirements.implementation_action: Issue the core_autonomous_runtime bridge release only after Rows321-347 and the existing Character-to-Image vertical slice pass.
requirements.title: Core Autonomous MaskFactory Bridge Release

workstreams.objective: Publish, verify, pin, and invalidate immutable MaskFactory integration releases.
Implement strict request/result ports for package and live-service access without authority inference.
Verify the frozen authority crosswalk, operational certificates, promotion gates, and revocation.
Preserve multi-character ownership, geometry, derived-mask lineage, protected regions, and bounded repair.
Provide health, idempotency, circuit breaking, cache invalidation, event replay, and recovery.
Exchange consumer requirements, adoption receipts, compatibility evidence, and non-mutating feedback.
Prove contracts, faults, observability, App projections, integrated execution, and core release.
